metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen the AC2 oracle to structurally prove single-source-of-truth, e.g. assert that both sidecar modules reference the same object (`plan_sidecar.RETAIN_PER_TICKET is code_sidecar.RETAIN_PER_TICKET`) or that only one definition site exists (`grep -rn 'RETAIN_PER_TICKET\s*=' src/rebar/llm | wc -l` equals 1).
findings.finding: AC2's stated oracle ('a test asserting both prune paths honor the same value') is weaker than AC2's stated criterion ('The cap is one named constant governing both paths'). A mirrored-constants implementation (two separate variables both set to 50) satisfies the oracle but violates the 'one named constant' structural claim, meaning the AC can be green while the criterion is false.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'The cap is one named constant governing both plan- and code-review prune paths. Oracle: a test asserting both prune paths honor the same value.'
findings.suggested_fix: AC2 oracle: 'a test asserting `plan_review.sidecar.RETAIN_PER_TICKET is code_review.sidecar.RETAIN_PER_TICKET` (import identity), confirming a single definition site; plus `grep -c "RETAIN_PER_TICKET\s*=" src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` should equal 1 (only one assignment exists in the codebase).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge and scope the missing prune implementation in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py`: add a `prune()` function mirroring plan_review's and wire it into `emit()` — the prune path does not currently exist and cannot be updated by a constant-value change alone.
findings.finding: The plan asserts there is an existing 'code-review prune path' in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` that needs only a constant update, but the file contains no `prune()` function and no `RETAIN_PER_TICKET` constant — the prune path must be written from scratch and wired into `emit()`.
findings.location: Scope section: `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` — 'ensure the same cap governs the code-review prune (shared or mirrored constant)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: 'Note: `code_review/sidecar.py` has no existing prune path — this change must add a `prune()` function (mirroring plan_review/sidecar.prune) and call it from `emit()` after `append_event`, in addition to declaring the shared constant.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge in Scope that `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` has no existing `prune()` function or `RETAIN_PER_TICKET` constant; explicitly scope the creation of both and the wiring of a `prune()` call into `code_review/sidecar.emit()` as a new-behavior addition (not merely an update to an existing path).
findings.finding: src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py contains no prune() function and no RETAIN_PER_TICKET constant — the 'code-review prune path' the plan names as an existing edit target does not exist; wiring it in requires creating both the function and a call from emit(), which is new behavior (CREATION), not an update.
findings.suggested_fix: Update the Scope entry for code_review/sidecar.py to read: 'Add RETAIN_PER_TICKET (shared with or mirrored from plan_review/sidecar.py) and a prune() function modelled on plan_review/sidecar.prune(); wire it as a best-effort call in emit() after append_event — mirroring the plan-review emit() pattern.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `prune()` function to `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` (mirroring the plan-review pattern) and call it from `code_review/sidecar.emit()` after `append_event`, so the retention cap actually bounds code-review sidecar growth — a shared/mirrored constant alone has no effect without a calling prune path.
findings.finding: The code_review/sidecar.py file has no prune() function and its emit() makes no prune call — there is no 'code-review prune path' to govern with a shared or mirrored constant. The plan's mechanism for the code-review half (add/share the constant) is incomplete: a constant with no calling code does not bound code-review sidecar growth.
findings.location: What / Scope section — 'src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py — ensure the same cap governs the code-review prune'
findings.suggested_fix: In code_review/sidecar.py: (1) define RETAIN_PER_TICKET = 50 (or import it from plan_review.sidecar); (2) add a prune() function identical in structure to plan_review/sidecar.prune() filtering on f"-{EVENT_TYPE}.json"; (3) call prune(target_ticket, repo_root=repo_root) at the end of emit() (best-effort, exception-swallowed), mirroring line 63 of plan_review/sidecar.py.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify in Scope/AC that `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` requires a NEW `prune()` function (mirroring the plan-review implementation), that it must be called from `emit()` after `append_event`, and that it should follow the same best-effort/exception-swallowing convention.
findings.finding: The code-review sidecar (`src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py`) has no `prune` function and no `RETAIN_PER_TICKET` constant — confirmed by tool search. The plan's Scope says 'ensure the same cap governs the code-review prune' and AC2 oracles 'a test asserting both prune paths honor the same value,' treating a prune path as already existing. It does not exist. The plan therefore fails to specify: (1) that a new `prune()` function must be authored for this file, (2) where in `emit()` it should be called, and (3) whether it should follow the same best-effort/exception-swallowing convention as the plan-review counterpart.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope bullet for `code_review/sidecar.py`: 'Add a `prune(ticket_id, *, keep=RETAIN_PER_TICKET, repo_root=None)` function mirroring `plan_review/sidecar.prune` (same git-rm logic, same best-effort/exception-swallowing pattern) and call it from `emit()` immediately after the `append_event` call, returning `True` on success regardless of prune outcome.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the code-review prune path: `code_review/sidecar.py` currently has no `RETAIN_PER_TICKET` constant and no `prune()` function — either (a) explicitly scope adding a prune function to that module as a new deliverable, or (b) narrow AC2 to cover only the plan-review prune path (the only existing one) and remove the code-review prune claim from Scope.
findings.finding: The plan's Scope and AC2 claim a 'code-review prune path' exists in `code_review/sidecar.py` and needs its constant aligned — but that file has neither a `RETAIN_PER_TICKET` constant nor a `prune()` function. Proposing a 'shared constant governs both prune paths' when one path doesn't yet exist is premature abstraction; AC2's oracle ('a test asserting both prune paths honor the same value') would require adding new production code that the plan never acknowledges as a new addition.
findings.location: Scope section ('src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py — ensure the same cap governs the code-review prune') and AC2 ('The cap is one named constant governing both plan- and code-review prune paths')
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, replace 'ensure the same cap governs the code-review prune (shared or mirrored constant)' with an explicit statement: 'Add a `RETAIN_PER_TICKET = 50` constant and a `prune()` call in `code_review/sidecar.py`'s `emit()`, mirroring the plan-review sidecar pattern.' Then update AC2's oracle to describe the new test accordingly. Alternatively, if code-review growth is not a current concern, remove the code-review prune from scope entirely and narrow AC2 to plan-review only.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align Scope and AC2: replace 'shared or mirrored constant' in Scope with 'single shared constant (imported by both prune paths)' to match the intent stated in Approach rationale and AC2.
findings.finding: Scope says 'shared or mirrored constant' (allowing two identical constants, one per file) but AC2 requires 'one named constant governing both … prune paths' (implying a single shared import). An implementer following the Scope's 'mirrored' path would fail AC2; an implementer following AC2 strictly might conflict with the Scope's looser wording.
findings.location: Scope section ('shared or mirrored constant') vs AC2 ('one named constant governing both … prune paths')
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, change 'shared or mirrored constant' to 'single shared constant imported by both sidecar modules'. This eliminates the ambiguity and aligns Scope with Approach rationale ('A single shared constant') and AC2 ('one named constant governing both … prune paths').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Scope section that the constant will be defined in exactly one place (e.g. a shared constants module or one sidecar imported by the other) rather than duplicated, so structural coupling prevents divergence rather than relying solely on the AC2 test.
findings.finding: The plan says 'shared or mirrored constant' without specifying whether the implementation will use a single imported constant (one source of truth) or two independently defined constants that happen to match. A mirrored approach allows silent divergence in future edits — the AC2 test catches it at test-time, but does not prevent it structurally.
findings.suggested_fix: Define `RETAIN_PER_TICKET = 50` in one location (e.g. `src/rebar/llm/review_constants.py` or at the top of `plan_review/sidecar.py`) and import it in `code_review/sidecar.py`. Update the Scope section to specify this import relationship explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default implementation approach in the Scope section (e.g. 'import the constant from `plan_review/sidecar.py` into `code_review/sidecar.py`; do not duplicate it') so the executor need not choose.
findings.finding: The Scope section leaves the structural implementation choice open with 'shared or mirrored constant', which is a real design decision with different future-maintainability implications (a shared import enforces single-source-of-truth; a mirrored copy can silently drift). The plan should state the default so the executor need not decide.
findings.location: Scope — `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` bullet: 'ensure the same cap governs the code-review prune (shared or mirrored constant)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'shared or mirrored constant' with 'a single constant defined in `plan_review/sidecar.py` (or a shared constants module) and imported by `code_review/sidecar.py`, so there is exactly one definition site.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
